elements.text: 5302.3: Third-party verification service providers:
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: employment and income verifications (06/03/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General requirements for third-party service provider verifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Employment and income verifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 10-day pre-closing verifications (PCV)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General requirements for third-party service provider verifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: Employment, income and 10-day PCVs obtained through third-party verification service
elements.label: text

elements.text: providers are acceptable if the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The verifications must be received by the originator directly from the third-party
elements.label: text

elements.text: verification service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If any required information is missing, the Seller must obtain additional documentation to
elements.label: text

elements.text: supplement the third-party verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller is responsible for the accuracy and integrity of the information provided by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: third-party verification service providers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller must verify that all third-party verification service providers have procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: in place to comply with quality control requests for reverification from Freddie Mac, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller and/or Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Employment and income verifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: A copy of the verification must be retained in the Mortgage file and:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Employment and income verifications must contain sufficient information to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: stable monthly income as required by Topic 5300
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the verification is completed using employment and/or income information from an
elements.label: text

elements.text: electronic database, the verification must evidence that the information in the database is
elements.label: text

elements.text: no more than 35 days old
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 5102.4 for more information about the age of documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Mortgages that use automated income assessment using employed income data
elements.label: text

elements.text: In lieu of the requirements of this section, Mortgages that use automated income
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment using employed income data must comply with the requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5303.4(c) if the Last Feedback Certificate indicates Seller’s eligibility for representation
elements.label: text

elements.text: and warranty relief is “Eligible” or “Partial” as described in Section 5303.4(d)(ii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Mortgages that use automated income assessment with Loan Product Advisor using
elements.label: text

elements.text: account data
elements.label: text

elements.text: In lieu of the requirements in this section, Mortgages that use automated income
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment with Loan Product Advisor using account data must comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 5303.5(c) and/or 5305.2(c), as applicable, if the Last Feedback
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certificate indicates Seller’s eligibility for representation and warranty relief is “Eligible”
elements.label: text

elements.text: or “Partial” as described in Section 5303.5(d)(ii) and/or 5305.2(d)(ii), as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) 10-day PCVs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Verification of the Borrower’s current employment (10-day PCV) must be obtained in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a third-party verification service provider is used, employment information must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: verified and documented by the third-party verification service provider directly through the
elements.label: text

elements.text: employer’s electronic database or the electronic database of the employer’s third-party
elements.label: text

elements.text: payroll services provider.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The 10-day PCV, when required, must be obtained no earlier than 10 Business Days before
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Note Date and no later than the day before the Delivery Date. As stated above, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment verification must evidence that the information in the database is no more than
elements.label: text

elements.text: 35 days old.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The employment verification must contain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Name of Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Name of employer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower’s current employment status
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any additional information that was verified
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Date employment information was issued from the employer to the third-party
elements.label: text

elements.text: verification services provider (e.g., effective date, current as of date)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Date verification was issued to the Seller by third-party verification service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: The form used by the third-party verification service provider must contain the name and
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact information of the service provider.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under certain circumstances, the Taxpayer First Act requires that the taxpayer’s consent be
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: third party. If taxpayer consent is required under the Act, Sellers must ensure that the form of
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: information with and by any actual or potential owners of the Mortgage, as well as their service
elements.label: text

elements.text: providers, successors and assigns. The signed consent form must be obtained in a timely manner
elements.label: text

elements.text: and placed in the Mortgage file in accordance with Section 3301.1(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Tax return requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tax returns must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s signed copy of the U.S. federal income tax return(s) that were most
elements.label: text

elements.text: recently filed with the Internal Revenue Service (IRS)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A complete tax return, including all applicable schedules and forms (which includes all
elements.label: text

elements.text: W-2s, K-1s and 1099s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are acceptable alternatives for the Borrower’s signature on the tax return(s):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evidence the tax returns were filed electronically (e.g., signed Form 8879, IRS e-file
elements.label: text

elements.text: Signature Authorization or equivalent), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tax transcripts that validate the information on the unsigned tax returns, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A completed IRS Form 4506-C signed by the Borrower, or alternate form acceptable to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the IRS that authorizes the release of comparable tax information to a third party
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Requirements for all Mortgages with qualifying income documented with tax returns
elements.label: text

elements.text: The most recent federal income tax return is the last tax return, individual and/or business,
elements.label: text

elements.text: that was filed with the IRS by the Borrower and, if applicable, the Borrower’s business.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers are encouraged to always confirm with the Borrower that the tax returns provided are
elements.label: text

elements.text: the tax returns most recently filed with the IRS.

elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation and analysis. Refer to Section 5304.1(g) for additional guidance about self￾employed income and supporting documentation when the tax returns for the most recent

elements.text: 5302.5: Internal Revenue Service (IRS) Form 4506-C
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: requirements for all income and asset qualification
elements.label: text

elements.text: sources (06/03/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages that use automated income assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Borrowers whose income is used to qualify or whose assets are used as a basis for
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment of obligations pursuant to Section 5307.1 must sign Internal Revenue Service
elements.label: text

elements.text: (IRS) Form 4506-C or an alternate form acceptable to the IRS that authorizes the release of
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable tax information to a third party (e.g., IRS Form 8821). The Form 4506-C must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: signed no later than the Note Date and must be retained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If submitting the Form 4506-C to the IRS, the IRS must receive the Form 4506-C before its
elements.label: text

elements.text: expiration date. The Seller must retain in the Mortgage file the tax documentation received
elements.label: text

elements.text: back from the IRS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the requirements above, for Borrowers with income derived from sources in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Puerto Rico, Guam or the U.S. Virgin Islands that are exempt from federal income taxation
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Internal Revenue Code, the following requirements apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In lieu of a Form 4506-C, Borrowers with income derived from sources in Puerto Rico
elements.label: text

elements.text: must sign the most recent version of Commonwealth of Puerto Rico Form 2907 titled
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Request For Copy of the Return, Estate or Gift Certificate of Release” (Modelo SC 2907
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Solicitud De Copia De Planilla, Relevo De Herencia Y De Donacion”) for submission to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Puerto Rico Department of the Treasury, Internal Revenue Area
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers with income derived from sources in Guam or the U.S. Virgin Islands must
elements.label: text

elements.text: sign the Form 4506-C (or an alternate form that authorizes the release of comparable tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: information to a third party) for submission to the Guam Department of Taxation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Revenue or Virgin Islands Bureau of Internal Revenue, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Mortgages that use automated income assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: In lieu of requirements in this section, Mortgages that use automated income assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: using employed income data must comply with the requirements in Section 5303.4(f) if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Last Feedback Certificate indicates Seller’s eligibility for representation and warranty relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: is “Eligible” or “Partial” as described in Section 5303.4(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: In lieu of requirements in this section, Mortgages that use automated income assessment with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor using account data must comply with the requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5303.5(e) and/or 5305.2(e), as applicable, if the Last Feedback Certificate indicates Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility for representation and warranty relief is “Eligible” or “Partial” as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5303.5(d)(ii) and/or 5305.2(d)(ii), as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5302.6: Automated employment assessment with
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor®
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible employment earnings using account data
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible employment earnings using employed income data
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data submission requirements, representation and warranty relief eligibility and
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: Representation and warranty relief eligibility is contingent on the Seller’s compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of this chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Asset and income modeler (AIM) automated employment assessment provides Sellers with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the option to use Loan Product Advisor® to determine whether the Seller is eligible for relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: from enforcement of certain representations and warranties related to the Borrower’s current
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain the Borrower’s account or employed income data and submit all
elements.label: text

elements.text: required data and information to Loan Product Advisor. Based on the data submitted, Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Product Advisor will assess for representation and warranty relief eligibility and return the
elements.label: text

elements.text: results of the assessment on the Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible employment earnings using account data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Employment earnings, as described in Section 5303.1, are eligible for automated
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment assessment with Loan Product Advisor, except for Borrowers with business
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership interest(s) less than 25%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When using account data, the Borrower’s depository history must reflect a minimum of three
elements.label: text

elements.text: consecutive payroll deposits from the same payor(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Eligible employment earnings using employed income data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Employment earnings, as described in Section 5303.1, are eligible for automated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 5303: Employed Income
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 5303.1: Employed income (06/03/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements and guidance related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements for all employed income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Employment history requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-fluctuating employment earnings (base, entitlements, military reserve, National Guard)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fluctuating employment earnings (base fluctuating hourly earnings, additional fluctuating earnings)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Employment/income characteristics – requirements and guidance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples throughout this section are provided for illustrative purposes only and do not represent the only allowable scenario(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Requirements for all employed income
elements.label: text

elements.text: All employed income used for qualifying must meet the requirements and guidance of this chapter and Section 5301.1 for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: stable monthly income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Stability and continuance of income
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must determine that the amount of income used to qualify the Borrower is stable and complies with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for each income type. All income must be expected to continue or have documented continuance for at least three
elements.label: text

elements.text: years as described in this chapter. The Seller must analyze all income documentation while taking into consideration the
elements.label: text

elements.text: characteristics of the employed income (e.g., employment source, earnings type, income type and stability of the employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: history, including any gaps in employment).
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Borrower who has had different types of employment in the past may be considered to have stable income if the income
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount has remained at a consistent level. When evaluating a Borrower who has frequently changed jobs, the Seller must
elements.label: text

elements.text: focus on whether the changes have affected the Borrower’s ability to pay their obligations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Primary and secondary employment types
elements.label: text

elements.text: Primary employment is the primary source of the Borrower’s employed income whether derived from employment such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: full-time employment, part-time employment, and/or seasonal employment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Secondary employment is any type of employment (e.g., second part-time job or multiple jobs) that is in addition to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s primary employment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Employment history requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Primary employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: In most instances, the Borrower should have at least a two-year history of primary employment documented on Form 65,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Uniform Residential Loan Application, and verified in accordance with Topic 5300.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Borrowers who are active-duty members of the U.S. Armed Forces, a history of military employment is not required for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the employment to be considered stable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tenure with the same employer or in a similar industry lends support to the analysis of employment stability.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Borrower has less than a two-year history of primary employment, the Seller must provide its justification for
elements.label: text

elements.text: determining that the employment is stable, taking into consideration factors such as income and/or employment characteristics
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the overall layering of risk factors, including the Borrower’s demonstrated ability to repay obligations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples that may support less than a two-year history (see minimum history requirements below):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Example 1: For a Borrower returning to the workforce after a period of extended absence, for any reason, documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: is provided to support a stable employment history that directly preceded the extended absence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Example 2: For a Borrower new to the workforce, documentation is provided that supports the Borrower’s recent
elements.label: text

elements.text: attendance at school or in a training program prior to their current employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minimum history requirements for base fluctuating hourly earnings: For additional minimum history requirements (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: 12 months) for base fluctuating hourly earnings, refer to the history requirements in Section 5303.1(d), below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Secondary employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: In most instances, the Borrower should have at least a two-year history of secondary employment for the employment to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered stable. When a Borrower has less than a two-year secondary employment history but has at least a 12-month
elements.label: text

elements.text: history, the Seller may be able to justify and determine the employment is stable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples that may support less than a two-year history:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Example 1: The Borrower previously held a job with base non-fluctuating earnings working 40 hours per week for
elements.label: text

elements.text: multiple years; however, due to reasons such as position elimination, work force reduction, or illness, the Borrower is no
elements.label: text

elements.text: longer employed at this job and now works at multiple part-time jobs that are similar in hours and pay, when combined, to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the previous full-time job. Since the Borrower’s full-time employment ended 18 months ago, the length of employment at
elements.label: text

elements.text: each part-time job is in the range of 13 to 15 months. In this scenario, the Seller may be able to justify an employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: history of less than two years for the secondary and additional jobs provided the earnings are consistent and the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: has exhibited the ability to repay obligations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Example 2: The Borrower is employed as a school teacher. During the previous summer, the Borrower taught summer
elements.label: text

elements.text: school and is now starting summer school teaching for the current year. Although the two-year history is not yet fully
elements.label: text

elements.text: developed, given the job type and current employment situation, the Seller may be able to justify including the summer
elements.label: text

elements.text: school income, provided an accurate qualifying amount can be established and documented based on the previous and
elements.label: text

elements.text: current earnings. Additional documentation to determine the stable monthly income may be appropriate (e.g., how many
elements.label: text

elements.text: classes, how much, is it similar to prior year).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Non-fluctuating employment earnings (base, military, additional fixed earnings)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements for history, continuance, calculation and documentation for non-fluctuating employment earnings are outlined in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: tables below. Additional documentation may be required to determine the stable monthly income amount. Refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5303.1(e) for additional documentation and requirements that may apply based on employment characteristics.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Base non-fluctuating employment earnings
elements.label: text

elements.text: Earnings type: Base non-fluctuating employment earnings are stable and consistent earnings that may be salaried or hourly.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Base non-fluctuating earnings may include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exempt (salaried)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Military base (basic) pay
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-exempt (hourly) earnings. The pay rate and number of hours are reflected on an ongoing consistent basis for each pay
elements.label: text

elements.text: period and must be supported by the year-to-date (YTD) income. If the only reason the earnings fluctuate is because of
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional employed income (e.g., overtime), the base earnings are still considered non-fluctuating.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Minor fluctuations in hours
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minor variations in base hours on paystubs (e.g., Borrower clocked out a few minutes early) are acceptable and may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: treated as base non-fluctuating earnings when the variation is no more than an hour per week. Minor variations do not
elements.label: text

elements.text: automatically render the base earnings as fluctuating if the historical earnings support the level of pay.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: The pay frequency is weekly. The current YTD paystub shows 39.78 hours while the prior paystub shows 40
elements.label: text

elements.text: hours. The YTD income reasonably supports 40 hours per week of gross pay and the prior year W-2(s) support a
elements.label: text

elements.text: similar amount of pay.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Exception for non-exempt earnings: primary employment earnings with minimum required hours
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Borrowers with primary employment earnings that fluctuate but have a position with a minimum number of
elements.label: text

elements.text: required hours, the earnings may be considered non-fluctuating, and the minimum required hours are acceptable to use
elements.label: text

elements.text: for gross pay when following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller must obtain written documentation from the employer confirming the minimum required hours (i.e.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: written verification of employment (VOE), offer letter or equivalent documentation)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The documented minimum required hours must be supported by YTD income and prior year, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Only the minimum required hours may be considered non-fluctuating. The requirements for fluctuating employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: earnings apply to any additional hours used to qualify the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example (minimum required hours):
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower has been with the current employer as a nurse with a hospital for four months; prior to that, the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: was in nursing school. The written VOE verifies the Borrower works three 12-hour shifts each week, which is common
elements.label: text

elements.text: for this profession, and the YTD earnings support at least this level of gross pay. Based on this, it is acceptable to
elements.label: text

elements.text: consider these the minimum required hours and use 36 hours weekly gross pay for income calculation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Calculation For all income used to qualify the Borrower, the Seller must determine the pay frequency (weekly, bi￾weekly, semi-monthly, monthly, quarterly, annually) to accurately analyze and calculate the stable

elements.text: monthly income. All documentation in the Mortgage file must support the Seller’s income analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: and calculation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table describes the calculation methods the Seller must use to determine stable monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: income for base non-fluctuating employment earnings, taking into consideration the typical pay
elements.label: text

elements.text: periods of weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly and monthly.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Calculation of base non-fluctuating employment earnings
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pay period type Calculation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Weekly Multiply the weekly gross pay by 52 and divide by 12.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bi-weekly (every
elements.label: text

elements.text: two weeks)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Multiply the biweekly gross pay by 26 and divide by 12.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Semi-monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: (twice per month)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Multiply the semi-monthly gross pay by 24 and divide by 12.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Monthly Use the monthly gross pay.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Annual base non￾fluctuating salary
elements.label: text

elements.text: paid out over less
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: per year (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: teachers)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Divide annual gross pay by 12
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If the annual salary is paid out over 10 months of the year,
elements.label: text

elements.text: multiply the monthly base salary amount by 10 months and divide by 12.

elements.text: Income history and stability – requirements and guidance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The stability of fluctuating income is primarily determined based on historical earnings, so it is imperative that a sufficient
elements.label: text

elements.text: income history has been established. For this reason, most income types that fluctuate have a history requirement of two years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In certain instances, a shorter history may still be considered stable if the Seller provides sufficient supporting documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: justifying the determination of stability. When making this determination, the Seller must take into consideration factors such
elements.label: text

elements.text: as income and/or employment characteristics and the overall layering of risk factors, including the Borrower’s demonstrated
elements.label: text

elements.text: ability to repay obligations. The income history must be at least 12 months.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Temporary leave from an employer may encompass various circumstances (e.g., family and medical, short-term disability,
elements.label: text

elements.text: maternity, other temporary leaves with or without pay). Temporary leave is generally short in duration. The period of time that a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is on temporary leave may be determined by various factors such as applicable law, employer policies and short-term
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance policy and/or benefit terms. Leave ceases being considered temporary when the Borrower does not intend to return to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: current employer or does not have a commitment from the current employer to return to employment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The requirements and guidance for income while on temporary leave do not extend to employer-initiated actions such as furloughs
elements.label: text

elements.text: and layoffs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Chapter 5305 regarding long-term disability income if the Seller has knowledge the Borrower has applied for, is receiving
elements.label: text

elements.text: or will be receiving long-term disability benefits or long-term insurance benefits.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Determining qualifying income and Borrower capacity to meet obligations while on temporary leave
elements.label: text

elements.text: During a temporary leave, a Borrower’s income may be reduced and/or completely interrupted. The Seller must determine that
elements.label: text

elements.text: during and after the temporary leave the Borrower has capacity to repay the Mortgage and all other monthly obligations in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Topics 5100 through 5500. The Seller’s determination must be based on required documentation, Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: knowledge and available information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) For Borrowers returning to their current employer prior to or on the first Mortgage payment due date
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Borrowers returning to their current employer prior to or on the first Mortgage payment due date, the Seller may use for
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualifying income the Borrower’s pre-leave gross monthly income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) For Borrowers returning to their current employer after the first Mortgage payment due date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Borrowers returning to their current employer after the first Mortgage payment due date, the Seller may use for
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualifying income the Borrower’s gross monthly income amount being received for the duration of the temporary leave
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Borrowers returning to their current employer after the first Mortgage payment due date, in the event that the income
elements.label: text

elements.text: has been reduced or interrupted, the Seller may use for qualifying income the monthly reduced income amount (this
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount may be zero) being received for the duration of the leave combined with the Borrower’s available liquid assets, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary. Available liquid assets may be used as a partial or complete income supplement up to the amount of the income
elements.label: text

elements.text: reduction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The “Asset calculation for establishing the debt payment-to-income ratio” described in Section 5307.1(b) does not apply to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the calculation of assets as an income supplement when determining qualifying income and Borrower capacity to meet
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations while on temporary leave.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assets that are required for the transaction (e.g., Down Payment, Closing Costs and reserves) may not be considered as
elements.label: text

elements.text: available assets.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The total qualifying income must not exceed the Borrower’s pre-leave gross monthly income amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following documentation is required for all Borrowers on temporary leave:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation to verify the Borrower’s pre-leave income and employment in accordance with Topic 5300, regardless of leave
elements.label: text

elements.text: status
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Written statement, in the form of a signed letter or an e-mail directly from the Borrower, confirming the Borrower’s intent to
elements.label: text

elements.text: return to the current employer and the intended date of return
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation generated by current employer confirming the Borrower’s eligibility to return to the current employer after
elements.label: text

elements.text: temporary leave. Acceptable forms of employer documentation that the Seller may obtain from the Borrower include but are
elements.label: text

elements.text: not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ An employer-approved leave request,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Family and Medical Leave Act document or other documentation generated by the employer or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A third-party verifier on behalf of the employer
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the following documentation is required for Borrowers returning to the current employer after the first Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment due date:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation evidencing amount and duration of all temporary leave income being used to qualify the Borrower (e.g., short￾term disability benefits or insurance, sick leave benefits, temporarily reduced income from employer) that are being received
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the temporary leave
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All available liquid assets used to supplement the reduced income for the duration of the temporary leave must meet the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of and be verified in accordance with the Streamlined Accept Documentation or Standard Documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements, as applicable, listed in Section 5501.3
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A written rationale explaining the analysis used to determine the qualifying income, regardless of the underwriting path
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5303.4: Automated income assessment using employed income data
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible income types
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data submission requirements, representation and warranty relief eligibility and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Closed Loans data submission requirements, representation and warranty relief eligibility and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Internal Revenue Service (IRS) Form 4506-C requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: Representation and warranty relief eligibility is contingent on the Seller’s compliance with all requirements as set forth in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Automated income assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Asset and income modeler (AIM) automated income assessment using employed income data, provides Sellers with the option
elements.label: text

elements.text: to use certain Systems (as defined in Section 2401.1(b)) to determine whether the Seller is eligible for relief from enforcement
elements.label: text

elements.text: of certain representations and warranties related to the Borrower’s income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain the Borrower’s employed income data and submit all required data and information to the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: System. Based on the data submitted, the System will assess for representation and warranty relief eligibility and return the
elements.label: text

elements.text: results of the assessment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The method to calculate income assessed by the applicable System may differ from income calculated using the methods
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Chapter 5303.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Automated income assessment of Closed Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers with access to Freddie Mac’s application programming interface (API) for automated income assessment with Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Product Advisor® using employed income data may submit data from a Closed Loan (an existing Mortgage in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer or a Related Third Party has a beneficial ownership interest as defined in Section 2402.2(b)(iii)) for assessment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller is eligible for income representation and warranty relief for a Closed Loan, the API response file will return a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Portable Document Format (PDF) document indicating eligibility (the “AIM Payroll API R&W Assessment”). The Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: should contact its Freddie Mac representative or Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE to request Freddie Mac’s approval to
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit Closed Loan data to the API.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The method to calculate income assessed by the applicable System may differ from income calculated using the methods
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Chapter 5303.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible income types
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following tables describes income types that are eligible for an automated income assessment using employed income data:
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must have no knowledge, information or documentation that contradicts an expectation that the income will
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue for at least the next three years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must confirm that the information on the third-party verification report (“verification report”) or paystub(s) and W-2
elements.label: text

elements.text: form(s) is for the correct Borrower and employer, and all employed income data must be in U.S. dollars.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Temporary leave of absence
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event the Borrower has taken a temporary leave of absence from their employment:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When a verification report is obtained, the verification report must indicate that, as of the date of the verification report, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower has returned to work
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the employed income data is from the Borrower’s paystub(s) and W-2 form(s), the Seller must maintain
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation in the Mortgage file showing the Borrower has returned to work
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Age of documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the age of the documentation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When a verification report is obtained, the expiration date of the verification report reflected in feedback messaging on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Last Feedback Certificate complies with the requirement in Section 5102.4
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the employed income data has been obtained from the Borrower’s paystub(s) and W-2 form(s), the paystub and W-2
elements.label: text

elements.text: must comply with the requirements in Section 5102.4(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Verification reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a verification report is obtained, the method used to obtain the verification report must be an automated process where
elements.label: text

elements.text: the employed income data is accessed directly from an electronic database of employer-provided income information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The employed income data on the verification report cannot be obtained from a written, verbal or e-mail verification of
elements.label: text

elements.text: income performed by the service provider.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Data submission requirements, representation and warranty relief eligibility and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Data submission requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must submit the most current employed income data to the applicable System. If after the initial submission, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller obtains an updated verification report, paystub(s) or W-2 form(s), the Seller must resubmit the employed income data.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Loan Product Advisor submissions, the Seller must submit the income amount used to underwrite the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a verification report is submitted to Loan Product Advisor, the Seller must investigate and resolve any inconsistent or
elements.label: text

elements.text: contradictory information between the verification report, information contained in Form 65, Uniform Residential Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Application (including the Borrower’s income and employment representations), and the Mortgage file and, if applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: resubmit the correct information to Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Representation and warranty relief results on Feedback Certificate

elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Documentation requirements based on representation and warranty relief result on Feedback Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements

elements.text: (i) Closed Loans data submission requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Closed Loans with an automated income assessment, the Seller must:

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: 5303.5: Automated income assessment with Loan Product Advisor® using
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: account data for employed income (06/03/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible income types
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data submission requirements, representation and warranty relief eligibility and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Internal Revenue Service (IRS) Form 4506-C requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: Representation and warranty relief eligibility is contingent on the Seller’s compliance with all requirements as set forth in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Asset and income modeler (AIM) automated income assessment using account data, provides Sellers with the option to use Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Product Advisor®
elements.label: text

elements.text: to determine whether the Seller is eligible for relief from enforcement of certain representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to the Borrower’s income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain the Borrower’s account data and submit all required data and information to Loan Product Advisor. Based
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the data submitted, Loan Product Advisor will assess for representation and warranty relief eligibility and return the results of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the assessment on the Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The method to calculate income assessed by Loan Product Advisor may differ from income calculated using the methods
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Chapter 5303.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible income types
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table describes income types that are eligible for an automated income assessment using account data:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible income types for an automated income assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible income type Ineligible employment/income characteristics
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following non-fluctuating employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: earnings as described in Section 5303.1(c):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Base non-fluctuating employment earnings
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Military earnings (base, entitlements, reserve,
elements.label: text

elements.text: National Guard)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5304.2: Automated income assessment using tax data
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: for self-employed income (06/03/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible self-employed income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data submission requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Representation and warranty relief eligibility and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: Asset and income modeler (AIM) automated income assessment using tax data provides
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers with the option to use certain Systems (as defined in Section 2401.1(b)) to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the Seller is eligible for relief from enforcement of certain representations and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warranties related to the Borrower’s self-employed income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain the Borrower’s tax data and submit all required data and information
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the applicable System. Based on the data submitted, the System will assess for
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation and warranty relief eligibility and return the results of the assessment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The method to calculate income assessed by the applicable System may differ from income
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated using the methods described in Section 5304.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Representation and warranty relief eligibility is contingent on the Seller’s compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: References in this section to “service provider” mean a third-party service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: designated by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Tax returns uploaded to a service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller obtains federal income tax returns from the Borrower, the Seller will upload
elements.label: text

elements.text: the tax returns to a service provider, which will produce a report (“Income Calculation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report”) consisting of data extracted from the Borrower’s tax returns.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may also request from the Internal Revenue Service (IRS), via the service
elements.label: text

elements.text: provider, a tax transcript of the Borrower’s federal individual income tax return(s). If
elements.label: text

elements.text: both tax returns and tax transcripts are obtained, Loan Product Advisor® will compare
elements.label: text

elements.text: data from the Borrower’s tax return to data from the tax transcript for assessment of tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: return data representation and warranty relief eligibility and return the results of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment on the Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Tax transcripts obtained from a service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternatively, the Seller may request from the IRS, via a service provider, a tax transcript
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Borrower’s federal individual income tax return(s). Loan Product Advisor will
elements.label: text

elements.text: retrieve the tax transcript data from the service provider and assess for income and data
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation and warranty relief eligibility and return the results of the assessment on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Freddie Mac Income Calculator using tax return or tax transcript data
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may enter data from the Borrower’s federal individual income tax returns or
elements.label: text

elements.text: tax transcripts into the Freddie Mac Income Calculator which will produce a Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Income Calculator Certificate that details the results of the Freddie Mac Income
elements.label: text

elements.text: Calculator submission, including the calculated income and representation and warranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: relief eligibility.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible self-employed income
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Tax return
elements.label: text

elements.text: Self-employed income is an eligible income type for an automated assessment using tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: return data if it is derived from the following business structures:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sole proprietorships reported on IRS Schedule C
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ S corporations reported on IRS Form 1120S (including compensation of officers
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported on IRS Form W-2), IRS Form 8825 and IRS Schedule K-1 (Form 1120S)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Partnerships reported on IRS Form 1065, IRS Form 8825 and IRS Schedule K-1
elements.label: text

elements.text: (Form 1065)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Tax transcript

elements.text: (c) Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must have been self-employed and received income from the same eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: income source (i.e., business) on the Income Calculation Report, the Freddie Mac Income
elements.label: text

elements.text: Calculator Certificate or Form 91, Income Calculations (or a similar alternative form as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 5304.1) for the most recent two years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s business has been in existence for less than five years or the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: has owned the business for less than five years, the Borrower’s federal income tax returns
elements.label: text

elements.text: or tax transcripts must reflect at least two years of receipt of income and/or losses from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the business. Income reported on the business tax returns but not on personal tax returns
elements.label: text

elements.text: must not be considered as stable monthly income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must have no knowledge, information or documentation that contradicts an
elements.label: text

elements.text: expectation that the income will continue for at least the next three years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Federal income tax return and tax transcript requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The tax returns or tax transcripts used in the automated income assessment must be the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s individual and business, if applicable, U.S. federal income tax return(s) that
elements.label: text

elements.text: were most recently filed with the IRS. Sellers are encouraged to always confirm with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower that the tax return(s) or tax transcripts provided are the most recent filed with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the IRS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The individual tax return year most recently filed with the IRS must correspond to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: business tax return year most recently filed with the IRS for all businesses (e.g., when the
elements.label: text

elements.text: most recently filed individual tax return year is 2024, the most recently filed business tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: return year must also be 2024).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The income and/or loss reflected in the federal income tax returns or tax transcripts must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be reported for the same business on the same tax form structure (e.g., the business
elements.label: text

elements.text: cannot be reported as a sole proprietorship and then converted to a partnership).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The tax returns must not be any of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Filed on a fiscal year basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Handwritten
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An amended return
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ From a U.S. Territory
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has amended their tax returns, then tax transcripts are not acceptable for
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment through the applicable System.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If using tax transcript(s), the assessment requires the most recent calendar year; a tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: extension is not allowed.
elements.label: text
